author: kkii
subject: rebrand UI to Mindy (slate + purple, Inter, M lettermark, dark-first)
body:
Pulls the brand identity from getmindy.ai and applies it across the
tracker so the internal BD tool feels native to Mindy:

- Palette: indigo accent → purple-500/600/700; zinc surfaces → slate
  (-50/100/800/900/950). CSS variables in globals.css mean every existing
  text-ink / bg-paper-card / border-ink/10 utility re-themes automatically.
- Display font: Geist → Inter (Mindy's font, loaded via Google Fonts CDN).
- Lettermark: "MI" → "M" in a purple gradient with soft purple glow.
- Wordmark: "Enterprise Pipeline" / "Market Assassin · SaaS" → "Mindy" /
  "Federal Pipeline Tracker".
- Page metadata: title + description + og + twitter tags rewritten to
  match Mindy's positioning. package.json name → "mindy-tracker".
- Dark-first: theme boot script now defaults to dark when no stored
  preference and the system hasn't expressed one (Mindy's site uses
  bg-slate-950 by default). Light mode still available via the toggle.
- README rebranded and expanded with the new architecture (Gmail OAuth
  setup, env vars, deployment notes).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: kkii
subject: wire Gmail OAuth + Send via Gmail integration
body:
New server-side routes power the connect flow and one-click sending:
- /api/auth/google + /callback (OAuth start + token exchange, CSRF state in
  encrypted iron-session cookie)
- /api/auth/status returns { connected, email }
- /api/auth/disconnect destroys the session
- /api/email/send builds an RFC 2822 message and submits via gmail.users.
  messages.send, returns { messageId, threadId, rfc822MessageId } for later
  threading and reply matching

Client side:
- New GmailConnection chip on /outreach acts as Connect / Disconnect toggle
  and surfaces "Sending as branden@trymindyai.com" once authorized
- ComposeModal gets a "Send via Gmail" button next to the existing
  "Open in Gmail" + "Log as sent" — disabled until connected + contact
  has an email. On success it stores the real gmail ids on the SentMessage
- Outreach page persists a `senderEmail` setting; gmailComposeUrl now
  includes it as the authuser param so the Open-in-Gmail flow always
  routes to the right Workspace account
- Typed fetch wrapper in lib/email/api-client.ts

Deps: googleapis 172, iron-session 8.

Requires four env vars in .env.local (gitignored): GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID,
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI, IRON_SESSION_PASSWORD.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: kkii
subject: add cold-email setup guide for trymindyai.com
body:
Step-by-step checklist covering Google Workspace signup, Namecheap DNS
records (MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC), domain verification, 14-day warmup plan,
ramp schedule for the first cold sends, and operational guardrails
(Postmaster Tools, DMARC tightening, bounce monitoring). Lives at
docs/EMAIL-SETUP.md so it's discoverable from the repo root.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: kkii
subject: add 40 backup contacts at Tier 1 organizations
body:
Researched secondary contacts at orgs where the primary lead might be
unreachable. New entries e-180 through e-219 are Tier 2 backups sitting
next to their primary in the file, with notes pointing back to the
primary's id. Skipped orgs that already had multiple contacts (NAPEX
cluster, WIPP, AFCEA, NACA, America's SBDC).

Real-world flags surfaced during research worth a follow-up pass:
- e-099 CBP / DMV VBOC has officially closed operations
- e-015 Fayetteville State VBOC primary may be stale (Robin Livingston
  now listed as Director instead of T.C. Bryant)
- e-110 Big Sky EDA VBOC primary likely the wrong person (Justin Rigby
  is the actual VBOC director; the seed's email mapped to the host-org
  Interim ED)
- e-102 MS State VBOC, e-105 WWBIC — primary name/email may be stale per
  current staff pages

Total contacts: 179 → 219.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: kkii
subject: redesign UI to modern-tech aesthetic with light + dark mode
body:
Drops the editorial-paper visual language (Fraunces serif, warm gold/rust/
sage palette, paper-grain noise overlay, period flourishes on page titles)
for a Linear/Vercel-style modern-tech look: Geist sans throughout, cool
zinc + indigo palette, brighter saturated status hues.

Dark mode is wired via Tailwind's `class` strategy backed by CSS variables
holding rgb triples — every existing `text-ink` / `bg-paper-card` /
`border-ink/10` utility re-themes automatically, no per-component edits.
ThemeToggle in the TopBar persists the choice (mi-tracker:theme:v1) and
respects prefers-color-scheme on first visit. An inline boot script in
app/layout.tsx applies the theme class before React hydrates to avoid
flash-of-wrong-theme.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: kkii
subject: add Kanban / Table / Grouped view switcher to Tracking page
body:
Three lenses on the same filtered contact list, all opening the same
ContactDetail panel on click. Table view is a sortable spreadsheet with
9 columns (click headers to sort, click again to flip direction). Grouped
view is collapsible sections defaulting to Category, with Tier and Owner
as alternate group-by axes. View choice persists in localStorage under
mi-tracker:view:v1. FilterBar still applies across every view.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: kkii
subject: backfill email / phone / LinkedIn for 12 Tier 1 APEX contacts
body:
Filled contactability gaps on top-tier NAPEX leadership and state APEX
directors where the previous seed had blank email/phone fields. 12 emails
added, 11 phones, 4 LinkedIn URLs. Three contacts use org-level program
inboxes (empireapex@nystec.com, kyapex@kstc.com, apex@schoolcraft.edu)
where personal addresses are not publicly published — fine for first
touch, swap to direct on reply. Scott Jensen (NVSBC, e-008) intentionally
left blank: no public email or phone, contact via website form or LinkedIn.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: kkii
subject: add /changelog route generated from git log
body:
New top-nav tab renders every commit as a date-grouped timeline with auto-
detected type chips (Add / Fix / Update / etc.), search, and trailer stripping
for Co-Authored-By lines. Entries come from lib/changelog.generated.json,
which scripts/generate-changelog.mjs writes from `git log --no-merges` at
prebuild/predev time. Also renames the Board nav label to "Tracking".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: kkii
subject: add outreach dashboard with templates, sequences, and sent-message tracking
body:
New /outreach route surfaces a follow-up queue (overdue / due today / upcoming),
a sent-message log with reply tracking, and editors for templates and sequence
cadence. Email sending is currently stubbed via "Open in Gmail" (compose URL)
plus "Log as sent" so the dashboard is useful before OAuth-based sending lands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: kkii
subject: add MBDA research and full-text VBOC/SBDC source files
body:
Adds the original full-length research markdown files at the repo
root. Note: vboc-contacts-may2026.md and sbdc-contacts-may2026.md
duplicate the condensed copies already at research/<same-name>.md
(those condensed versions remain in place). mbda-contacts-may2026.md
is new research; the ~35 MBDA Business Center contacts have NOT been
parsed into lib/seed.ts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: kkii
subject: add SBDC state directors from May 2026 research
body:
Append 63 SBDC state/territory directors (e-117 through e-179)
covering all 10 SBA regions. Includes 4 Texas regional + 5 California
regional leads since neither state has a single statewide SBDC head.
Existing ASBDC HQ entries (e-010 Rowe, e-011 Youngblut, e-012
Thibodeau) untouched.

Notable design: Byron Hicks (ASBDC 2025-2026 Board Chair) added as
parallel SBDC entry e-138, alongside existing APEX entry e-059 — same
person, two filterable program rows for the SBDC+APEX dual program he
runs at NC SBTDC. Tiffany Ford added as her own e-161 SBDC entry
(previously only referenced in e-089 ND APEX notes); she runs the
triple-program at UND (SBDC + APEX + VBOC).

Tiers: 21 Tier 1 (ASBDC Board officers + large-state SBDCs +
co-located APEX cross-sells at UA/UWF/IL DCEO/IEDC/MSU MI/OH Dept of
Dev/Kutztown/GMU/UMD/UH/UTSA/Texas Tech/Maricopa/UNO/UND/Boise State
+ LA Regional). 29 Tier 2 standard states. 13 Tier 3 small
states/territories.

18 co-located APEX+SBDC cross-sell accounts flagged in notes with
matching APEX entry IDs for outreach pairing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: kkii
subject: add VBOC contacts from May 2026 research
body:
Append 20 nationwide VBOC contacts (e-097 through e-116) covering all
10 SBA regions. Update e-014 in place (McNulty Center → Arsenal
Business and Technology Partnership rename, same Amy Amoroso, coverage
expanded to NJ/NY/PR/USVI). Update e-015 in place (added T.C. Bryant
as director, verified email/phone, coverage expanded to KY/NC/SC,
bumped to Tier 1 for Fort Liberty proximity).

Tiers: 10 Tier 1 (co-located APEX+VBOC cross-sells at ODU/UTA/UND/
Big Sky EDA/MS State, plus high-profile leaders like Wendy Baumann,
plus large mil clusters), 9 Tier 2 standard, 1 Tier 3 (Pacific
territories). Cross-sell hooks captured for 5 co-located APEX+VBOC
accounts.

Flagged: possible overlap between e-016 (Horstman/Georgia Southern)
and new e-103 (VECTR Center). Skipped existing e-013 (SC Citadel).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: kkii
subject: add APEX contacts from May 2026 research
body:
Append 68 nationwide APEX Accelerator contacts (e-029 through e-096)
to the seed array, sourced from research/apex-contacts-may2026.md.
Skips 6 existing duplicates (Beasley, Thomas, Dixon, VA/GMU, Berman,
Carrier). Tiers: 10 Tier 1 (NAPEX officers + FL/OH/TX/IN/KY/MI/NYC),
40 Tier 2 (standard states + OH sub-centers + tribal multi-state),
18 Tier 3 (territories + small states). High-priority transition
hooks captured in notes (Idaho host change, Maine new director,
Empire NY promotion, MA recompete, Montana interim, NYC SBS new ED).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
